title: China-to-Laos Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 for PV Inverters — DOSM / EDL / MEM Requirements in Laos
rows.target_requirement: DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology) adopts IEC-based standards for regulated electrical and electronic equipment in Laos, including IEC 62920 (Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment) and IEC 61000-3-2 (Limits for harmonic current emissions for equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase). EDL's technical review for grid-connected solar PV installations references harmonic emission limits consistent with IEC 61000-3-2. IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for PV power conversion equipment and is the relevant benchmark for conformity documentation when DOSM conducts product-level EMC review. There is no publicly available standalone Laos-specific EMC technical regulation for solar inverters identified as of June 2026; EMC requirements are embedded in IEC standards adopted by DOSM and referenced in EDL grid-connection technical review.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment (adopted as reference by DOSM for PV inverter conformity in Laos)
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (equipment with input current up to 16 A per phase) (referenced in EDL grid-connection harmonic requirements)
DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology, Lao PDR) — regulatory framework for conformity of regulated electrical products, including PV inverters
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC is assessed under GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which includes EMC test requirements aligned with China's domestic regulatory framework. EMC testing is performed at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. Although IEC 62920 draws on IEC 61000-series test methods that also inform Chinese domestic standards, GB/T 37408 EMC test scope and referenced limits are not harmonised with IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 as applicable in Laos under DOSM's IEC adoption framework. Chinese EMC certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 are not accepted as IEC 62920 or IEC 61000-3-2 compliance evidence for DOSM conformity review or EDL project submissions in Laos.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC test requirements under China's domestic framework
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC testing does not satisfy DOSM's IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 requirements as applicable in Laos. Exporters should obtain: (a) IEC 62920 EMC type-test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model or power-rating family; (b) IEC 61000-3-2 harmonic current emission test report for the model. Note that harmonic test results obtained under Chinese grid conditions (220/380 V) may need to be re-evaluated for Laos's 230/400 V grid conditions — confirm with the EDL project engineer or DOSM technical reviewer whether retesting under Laos grid conditions is required. Submit EMC evidence as part of the DOSM conformity documentation package and EDL project technical review submission.

mandatory_status.target: Required for DOSM conformity assessment of regulated PV inverter products and for EDL grid-connection technical review in Laos; IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 are the applicable IEC benchmarks under DOSM's framework
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408 EMC testing supports CQC/CGC voluntary certification in China; not a standalone mandatory market-access requirement for domestic China PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates do not satisfy DOSM Laos IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 conformity requirements. Obtain IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-3-2 type-test reports from an ILAC-accredited laboratory for the specific inverter model before submitting to DOSM conformity review or EDL project technical review. Confirm with DOSM and EDL whether Laos grid voltage conditions (230/400 V) require retesting compared to results obtained under 220/380 V Chinese grid conditions. Laos EMC regulatory requirements may evolve — verify the current DOSM regulated-product list and applicable standards with DOSM directly before shipment.

source.publisher: DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology), Ministry of Industry and Commerce, Lao PDR

rows.requirement: EDL / MEM Grid-Connection Technical Approval — Anti-Islanding (IEC 62116), 50 Hz / 230 V / 400 V Settings, and MEM Project Licensing
rows.target_requirement: Grid-connected solar PV installations in Laos require technical approval from EDL (Électricité du Laos) and EDL-T (EDL transmission subsidiary) for connection to the national grid, and MEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines) project licensing for renewable energy installations. EDL's grid-connection technical review references IEC standards, including IEC 62116 for anti-islanding protection (inverters must detect loss of mains and disconnect within a defined time), IEC 61727 for photovoltaic system grid-interface characteristics, and IEC 61000-series harmonic requirements. Laos's grid operates at 50 Hz, 230 V single-phase, 400 V three-phase — aligned with IEC standards. Inverter firmware protection thresholds and reconnection delays must be validated for Laos's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid parameters. Note: Laos is the 'battery of Southeast Asia'; its grid is hydropower-dominant with growing hybrid hydro-solar and floating solar capacity (e.g. Nam Ngum reservoir projects). EDL also exports electricity to Thailand, Vietnam, and neighbouring countries under BRI-linked grid interconnections, meaning EDL-T interconnection technical requirements may apply to larger projects.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures (referenced by EDL technical review for grid-connected PV)
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic (PV) systems — Characteristics of the utility interface (applicable to grid-interface requirements for PV inverters in Laos)
IEC 61000-3-2 — Electromagnetic compatibility — Limits for harmonic current emissions (referenced in EDL/MEM technical requirements for grid-connected inverters)
MEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines) Laos — Renewable Energy Development Strategy 2025 and project licensing requirements for grid-connected solar PV
EDL (Électricité du Laos) — Grid-connection technical review and connection agreement requirements for solar PV installations
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System, applicable to utility-scale PV via 10 kV and above), NB/T 32004-2018 (inverter technical specifications), and GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters, which superseded GB/T 37408-2019 in China's domestic market). These Chinese grid-connection documents are based on China's 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid parameters under GB/T 156. Laos operates at 230/400 V, 50 Hz — a 4–5% voltage difference that requires re-validation of firmware protection thresholds. Although Chinese-brand inverters are widely deployed in Laos under BRI-linked projects, this market prevalence does not constitute formal DOSM conformity or EDL/MEM technical approval. Chinese grid-connection certificates do not transfer to Laos EDL grid-connection acceptance.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages) — China domestic grid: 220 V single-phase, 380 V three-phase, 50 Hz
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T grid-connection documents are based on 220/380 V Chinese grid parameters and are not accepted as EDL/MEM Laos grid-connection evidence. Exporters must: (a) obtain IEC 62116 anti-islanding test report from an ILAC-accredited laboratory validated for 230/400 V, 50 Hz Laos grid parameters; (b) provide IEC 61727 grid-interface compliance evidence; (c) re-parameterise and document inverter firmware protection thresholds for Laos's 230/400 V grid — do not assume Chinese 220/380 V firmware settings transfer directly; (d) submit to EDL technical review as part of the MEM project licensing and connection application process; (e) for larger utility-scale or BRI-financed projects, confirm EDL-T interconnection technical requirements separately. Market prevalence of Chinese brands in Laos does not remove the requirement to obtain formal DOSM conformity and EDL/MEM technical approval for each new project.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected solar PV projects in Laos; MEM project licensing and EDL technical grid-connection approval are non-negotiable gates regardless of equipment brand origin or BRI financing
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China's domestic grid operator requirements (State Grid / Southern Power Grid) but does not transfer to Laos EDL grid-connection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EDL/MEM Laos grid-connection technical approval requires IEC-standard evidence (IEC 62116, IEC 61727) and firmware re-validation for Laos's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid — Chinese GB/T grid-connection certificates do not satisfy this gate. Re-parameterise inverter firmware for Laos grid parameters and document the configuration before project submission. Confirm current MEM licensing requirements, EDL technical review criteria, and EDL-T interconnection conditions directly with MEM and EDL before finalising the equipment compliance package, as regulatory requirements in Laos are evolving.

rows.requirement: DOSM Conformity Assessment and MEM Renewable Energy Project Licensing — Market-Access Gates for PV Inverters in Laos
rows.target_requirement: DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology, under Laos Ministry of Industry and Commerce) is the national standards body responsible for setting and adopting technical standards in Laos. DOSM adopts IEC-based standards for electrical and electronic equipment. Importers and installers of PV inverters in Laos are subject to DOSM conformity requirements for regulated electrical products. MEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines) administers renewable energy project licensing under the Electricity Law of Laos (Law No. 08/NA, 2011, as amended) and its implementing regulations. Grid-connected solar PV projects require MEM approval covering project licensing, technical specifications, and environmental review before EDL grid connection. The combination of DOSM product conformity and MEM project licensing constitutes the formal regulatory gate for solar PV inverters in Laos, regardless of whether the equipment is already commonly deployed in the market under BRI-linked projects.
target_standards: Laos Electricity Law No. 08/NA (2011, as amended) — primary law governing electricity generation, transmission, distribution, and connection in Laos
MEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines) Laos — renewable energy project licensing and technical review requirements
DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology) Laos — conformity assessment for regulated electrical products, adopting IEC-based standards
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems (adopted as reference standards by DOSM for PV equipment conformity)
rows.china_equivalent: China does not have a direct equivalent to DOSM's conformity assessment for Laos. Chinese PV inverters sold domestically in China undergo CQC or CGC voluntary certification under GB/T 37408 and NB/T 32004 frameworks. These Chinese voluntary certificates, while useful as supplementary documentation, are not substitutes for DOSM conformity assessment under Laos's own IEC-aligned regulatory framework. For MEM project licensing, Chinese NEA (National Energy Administration) distributed or utility-scale PV project approvals are China-domestic authorities with no legal standing in Laos regulatory process. Chinese state-owned enterprise involvement in BRI-financed Laos projects does not remove the requirement for DOSM conformity or MEM licensing of equipment used in those projects.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — basis for CQC/CGC voluntary certification in China
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
China NEA distributed PV policy — applicable to Chinese domestic project approvals only, not transferable to Laos MEM licensing
rows.gap: Gap: DOSM conformity and MEM project licensing are both required regulatory gates for solar PV inverters in Laos and are separate from any Chinese domestic certification or project approval. Exporters and project developers must: (a) confirm current DOSM regulated-product scope and applicable IEC-based conformity requirements for the specific inverter model; (b) obtain MEM project licensing covering technical specifications before grid connection; (c) ensure inverter IEC 62109-1/-2 safety evidence and IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence is available to support both DOSM conformity and MEM/EDL technical review; (d) not assume that BRI project precedents or Chinese brand prevalence in the Laos market grants exemption from DOSM or MEM formal processes. Regulatory capacity in Laos is developing — confirm current requirements with DOSM and MEM directly.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory — DOSM conformity for regulated electrical products and MEM project licensing are both required before grid connection; EDL will not connect a project without MEM approval
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese CQC/CGC voluntary certificates and NEA project approvals are China-domestic instruments with no legal standing in Laos DOSM or MEM processes

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Both DOSM conformity assessment and MEM project licensing are formal gates for solar PV inverters entering the Laos market or connecting to the EDL grid. Chinese domestic certifications (CQC/CGC under GB/T 37408) and Chinese NEA project approvals do not substitute for these Laos requirements. Engage with DOSM directly to confirm current regulated-product scope and IEC conformity documentation requirements for the specific inverter model before shipment. Confirm MEM licensing requirements and EDL technical review process with MEM and EDL before project commitment.

source.publisher: MEM (Ministry of Energy and Mines), Lao PDR

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — DOSM Conformity and EDL / MEM Technical Review in Laos
rows.target_requirement: DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology, Lao PDR) adopts IEC 62109-1 (Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters) as the applicable safety standards for solar PV inverters in Laos. Third-party type-test evidence from an ILAC-accredited laboratory against IEC 62109-1/-2 is the expected conformity documentation for DOSM product review and EDL/MEM project technical review. IEC 62109-1 sets general safety requirements for all PV power converters; IEC 62109-2 adds inverter-specific requirements and cannot be assessed independently of Part 1. Project owners and procurement specifications for grid-connected solar PV in Laos — particularly utility-scale projects with international financing or EDL procurement — typically require IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates as a minimum safety evidence threshold.
target_standards: IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters (adopted by DOSM Laos; referenced in EDL/MEM technical review for grid-connected PV)
IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements (parent standard; implicitly applicable whenever IEC 62109-2 is assessed)
DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology, Lao PDR) — conformity assessment framework for regulated electrical products including PV inverters
rows.china_equivalent: China's primary standard for PV grid-connected inverter safety is GB/T 37408-2021 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters), which covers classification, environmental conditions, safety, electrical performance, EMC, and markings. GB/T 37408 is a China domestic standard assessed at CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. CQC (China Quality Certification Centre) and CGC (China General Certification Centre) offer voluntary PV inverter certification based on GB/T 37408 and NB/T 32004. While GB/T 37408 shares conceptual safety intent with IEC 62109-1/-2, it is not harmonised with IEC 62109 and is not accepted by DOSM or Laos EDL/MEM project technical review as equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence. This gap applies even where the same Chinese-brand inverter has already been deployed in other Laos BRI projects — each project and each product conformity submission is assessed on its documentation.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CQC/CGC voluntary PV inverter certification — China domestic voluntary schemes based on GB/T 37408; not accepted as IEC 62109-1/-2 equivalent in Laos
rows.gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 safety certification does not satisfy DOSM Laos IEC 62109-2 type-test requirement or the safety evidence expectation of EDL/MEM project technical review. Exporters must obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates from an ILAC-accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model or family being supplied to the Laos project. Existing Chinese safety certificates based solely on GB/T 37408 should be supplemented with IEC 62109-1/-2 test reports before submitting equipment documentation for DOSM conformity review, MEM project licensing, or EDL technical review. Note that IEC 62109-1 (2010) has been in force for over a decade and major Chinese inverter manufacturers already hold IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates from international test laboratories (TÜV, Bureau Veritas, Intertek, etc.) — confirm the certificate covers the specific model and power rating being supplied to Laos.

mandatory_status.target: Required for DOSM conformity assessment of PV inverters as regulated electrical products in Laos; IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence is expected by EDL and MEM in project technical review and is a procurement baseline for utility-scale and internationally financed projects
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — GB/T 37408-2021 is a recommended national standard (GB/T designation); CQC/CGC voluntary certification is available but not required under Chinese law for domestic market PV inverters

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A solar PV inverter holding only Chinese GB/T 37408 certification cannot satisfy DOSM Laos IEC 62109-1/-2 conformity requirements or EDL/MEM project safety evidence expectations. Plan for IEC 62109-1/-2 type testing by an ILAC-accredited laboratory — many major Chinese inverter manufacturers already hold such certificates, so first confirm whether an existing certificate covers the specific model and power rating to be supplied to Laos before commissioning new testing. Verify current DOSM regulated-product scope and applicable IEC standard editions with DOSM directly before shipment, as Laos regulatory capacity and product-scope determinations continue to develop.

source.publisher: DOSM (Department of Standards and Metrology), Ministry of Industry and Commerce, Lao PDR
